quote: A dictionary is never finished; it reflects the language at a given moment, and the moment is always moving.
Dictionaries are like watches; the worst is better than none, and the best cannot be expected to go quite true.
To make a dictionary is to record the life of a language.
The dictionary is the only place where success comes before work.
A dictionary is the universe in alphabetical order.
Lexicography is the branch of learning that has to do with the making of dictionaries. It is sometimes said to be an art and sometimes a craft.
source: Dictionaries: The Art and Craft of Lexicography

quote: Words are the tokens of our thoughts; a dictionary is the treasury of our language.
The vocabulary of a language is a mirror of the culture that speaks it.
A living language is a language that is still changing; a dead dictionary is one that has stopped recording those changes.
Dictionaries are the compost heaps of the language; they turn the refuse of the past into the soil of the future.
